commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Maintainer review should decide the public source-lock API, generated lock workflow, and stacked PR order; there is no narrow ClawSweeper repair to queue.; Cleared: The diff broadens plugin materialization but uses exact specs, HTTPS/fixed-output hashes, and no runtime package-manager install; no concrete security defect was found. (sha=1696121f092d5d686cf1ec9632264eea3f45a19a)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds shrinkwrap-backed runtime plugin materialization, a new `programs.openclaw.runtimePluginSources` Home Manager option, generated locks for additional official plugin ids, validation scripts/checks, and revised runtime plugin docs.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR, not a bug report. Source inspection verifies current main lacks `runtimePluginSources` and PR head adds it.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainer review should decide the public source-lock API, generated lock workflow, and stacked PR order; there is no narrow ClawSweeper repair to queue.

target.title: feat: materialize shrinkwrapped runtime plugins
target.body:
# Human written summary:
The intent of this change is, as written by a human:
> all users want is to be able to use nix-openclaw with plugins the same way that they do with upstream openclaw, but declaratively. its that simple.

_The rest of this PR was written by GPT-5 Codex-high, running in the codex harness. Full environment + prompt history appear at the end._

for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels

# Changes
- Keeps the generated-id path simple: `programs.openclaw.runtimePlugins = [ "discord" "copilot" ];` still selects packageable official OpenClaw runtime plugin ids from the pinned OpenClaw release.
- Adds `programs.openclaw.runtimePluginSources` for arbitrary exact npm and ClawHub runtime plugin packages:
  - `id`
  - exactly one of `spec` or `url`
  - `hash`, defaulting to `lib.fakeHash`
  - optional `npmDepsHash`, added only when shrinkwrap materialization asks for it
- Resolves `npm:<package>@<version>` and `clawhub:<package>@<version>` inside a fixed-output Nix build, then validates the result as an OpenClaw runtime plugin root.
- Reuses the same builder for generated locks and user-supplied locked sources; activation and OpenClaw runtime never run `openclaw plugins install`, npm, or ClawHub resolution.
- Tightens validation:
  - direct `url` sources must be HTTPS npm-pack tarballs;
  - optional runtime dependencies no longer pass as bundled unless their roots are actually present;
  - ambiguous/invalid source records name the bad entries.
- Rewrites the README runtime plugin section around upstream OpenClaw install surfaces and the nix-openclaw equivalents: source schemes first, the shrinkwrap/bundled-deps rule second, lower-level supported paths below, and the fake-hash flow for locked sources.

# Tests
- PASS: `node --check nix/scripts/openclaw-runtime-plugin-fetch-source.mjs && node --check nix/scripts/openclaw-runtime-plugin-install.mjs && node --check nix/scripts/openclaw-runtime-plugin-prepare-npm.mjs && node --check nix/scripts/check-openclaw-runtime-plugin-installer.mjs`
- PASS: `node nix/scripts/check-openclaw-runtime-plugin-installer.mjs nix/scripts/openclaw-runtime-plugin-install.mjs`
- PASS: `git diff --check`
- PASS: `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` after the README-only documentation cleanup
- PASS: `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#checks.aarch64-darwin.default-instance`
- PASS: `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#checks.x86_64-linux.default-instance`
- PASS: arbitrary npm source build for `npm:@openclaw/diagnostics-prometheus@2026.6.1` with only source `hash`
- PASS: arbitrary ClawHub source build for `clawhub:@openclaw/whatsapp@2026.6.1` with source `hash` and `npmDepsHash`
- PASS: `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#checks.aarch64-darwin.ci`
- PASS: `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#checks.x86_64-linux.ci`

# Risks
- This expands the user-facing Home Manager API with a new locked-source option. The option is intentionally narrow and Nix-shaped.
- Exact versions for `npm:` and `clawhub:` specs are enforced by the fixed-output resolver build, not by Nix option type regex at evaluation time.
- Git, local path, npm-pack local files, and marketplace installs remain out of this declarative source API until they have equally fixed Nix source identities.
- Weixin, Yuanbao, and WeCom still need upstream package changes if maintainers want them in generated `runtimePlugins`: publish `npm-shrinkwrap.json` or bundle runtime dependencies.

# Follow-ups
- Optional: add a lock helper command later if users need a friendlier way to fill `hash` and `npmDepsHash`; not required for the Nix convention because `lib.fakeHash` already works.
- Optional: upstream PRs to unshrinkwrapped plugin packages after maintainers choose which owners/sources to chase.

# Prompt History

## Environment
Harness: codex
Model: GPT-5 Codex
Thinking level: high
Terminal: zsh
System: macOS 26.5 / Darwin 25.5.0 arm64

## Prompts
| ISO-8601 | Prompt |
| --- | --- |
| 2026-06-05T00:00:00+02:00 | `Goal: design the next architecture step for nix-openclaw support of OpenClaw's upstream runtime npm/ClawHub plugin model. Do not patch repos yet. Do not create worktrees.` |
| 2026-06-05T00:00:00+02:00 | `all users want is to be able to use nix-openclaw with plugins the same way that they do with upstream openclaw, but declaratively. its that simple.` |
| 2026-06-05T00:00:00+02:00 | `can we package them using shrinkwrap or something? can we be clever and say if its shrinkwrapped then we support it otherwise fuck off? because shrinkwrap lets you install declaratively` |
| 2026-06-05T00:00:00+02:00 | `sure thats fine. okay then i think we have everyhting, simple as possible user config, as few knobs as possible, we support everything that has shrinkwrapped, we have a config, etc, this should be clear?` |
| 2026-06-05T00:00:00+02:00 | `i added a bunch of comments on the repo. the readme is still really bad and really doesn't make this friendly user-facing at all. compare with upstream openclaw plugin docs and how they explain it.` |
| 2026-06-06T00:00:00+02:00 | `we should also use the lib.fakehash thing so the user doesn't supply anything more than that? follow usual nix conventions? adversarial review should be checking for stuff like "is this nix like"` |

Older prompt timestamps above are normalized because this work resumed from a compacted Codex thread; quoted prompts are copied from the thread context available to the agent.

# Human written summary:
The intent of this change is, as written by a human:

# Changes

  - `id`

  - optional `npmDepsHash`, added only when shrinkwrap materialization asks for it

- Keeps the generated-id path simple: `programs.openclaw.runtimePlugins = [ "discord" "copilot" ];` still selects packageable official OpenClaw runtime plugin ids from the pinned OpenClaw release.
- Adds `programs.openclaw.runtimePluginSources` for arbitrary exact npm and ClawHub runtime plugin packages:
  - `id`
  - exactly one of `spec` or `url`
  - `hash`, defaulting to `lib.fakeHash`
  - optional `npmDepsHash`, added only when shrinkwrap materialization asks for it
- Resolves `npm:<package>@<version>` and `clawhub:<package>@<version>` inside a fixed-output Nix build, then validates the result as an OpenClaw runtime plugin root.
- Reuses the same builder for generated locks and user-supplied locked sources; activation and OpenClaw runtime never run `openclaw plugins install`, npm, or ClawHub resolution.
- Tightens validation:
  - direct `url` sources must be HTTPS npm-pack tarballs;
  - optional runtime dependencies no longer pass as bundled unless their roots are actually present;
  - ambiguous/invalid source records name the bad entries.
- Rewrites the README runtime plugin section around upstream OpenClaw install surfaces and the nix-openclaw equivalents: source schemes first, the shrinkwrap/bundled-deps rule second, lower-level supported paths below, and the fake-hash flow for locked sources.

# Tests
- PASS: `node --check nix/scripts/openclaw-runtime-plugin-fetch-source.mjs && node --check nix/scripts/openclaw-runtime-plugin-install.mjs && node --check nix/scripts/openclaw-runtime-plugin-prepare-npm.mjs && node --check nix/scripts/check-openclaw-runtime-plugin-installer.mjs`
- PASS: `node nix/scripts/check-openclaw-runtime-plugin-installer.mjs nix/scripts/openclaw-runtime-plugin-install.mjs`
- PASS: `git diff --check`
- PASS: `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` after the README-only documentation cleanup
- PASS: `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#checks.aarch64-darwin.default-instance`
- PASS: `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#checks.x86_64-linux.default-instance`
- PASS: arbitrary npm source build for `npm:@openclaw/diagnostics-prometheus@2026.6.1` with only source `hash`
- PASS: arbitrary ClawHub source build for `clawhub:@openclaw/whatsapp@2026.6.1` with source `hash` and `npmDepsHash`
- PASS: `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#checks.aarch64-darwin.ci`
- PASS: `nix build --accept-flake-config --no-link .#checks.x86_64-linux.ci`

# Risks
- This expands the user-facing Home Manager API with a new locked-source option. The option is intentionally narrow and Nix-shaped.
- Exact versions for `npm:` and `clawhub:` specs are enforced by the fixed-output resolver build, not by Nix option type regex at evaluation time.
- Git, local path, npm-pack local files, and marketplace installs remain out of this declarative source API until they have equally fixed Nix source identities.
- Weixin, Yuanbao, and WeCom still need upstream package changes if maintainers want them in generated `runtimePlugins`: publish `npm-shrinkwrap.json` or bundle runtime dependencies.

# Follow-ups
- Optional: add a lock helper command later if users need a friendlier way to fill `hash` and `npmDepsHash`; not required for the Nix convention because `lib.fakeHash` already works.
- Optional: upstream PRs to unshrinkwrapped plugin packages after maintainers choose which owners/sources to chase.
